rows.rationale: Glutamate racemase uses cysteine acid-base racemization chemistry, which is not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
3-hydroxydecanoyl-ACP dehydratase performs dehydration chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Betaine-aldehyde dehydrogenase performs NAD-dependent aldehyde oxidation outside the current seed fingerprints.
Bis(5'-adenosyl)-triphosphatase uses HIT-superfamily histidine nucleotidyl hydrolase chemistry not represented by the current metal or Ser-His-Asp/Glu hydrolase seeds.
Cytochrome L-lactate dehydrogenase uses flavin-linked lactate oxidation to pyruvate, matching the flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Vanillyl-alcohol oxidase is a FAD-dependent alcohol redox enzyme, fitting the broad flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed rather than oxygen-transfer chemistry.
Quinoprotein glucose dehydrogenase uses PQQ-dependent glucose oxidation outside the current flavin, heme, cobalamin, and hydrolase seed fingerprints.
FAD-independent aldehyde dehydrogenase uses molybdenum/sulfur aldehyde oxidation chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Pyruvate dehydrogenase uses thiamine-diphosphate acyl-transfer chemistry outside the current hydrolase and redox seed fingerprints.
Aerobic carbon monoxide dehydrogenase uses molybdenum-copper CO oxidation chemistry outside the current hydrolase, flavin, and heme seed fingerprints.
2,4-dienoyl-CoA reductase performs flavin-mediated electron transfer from NADPH toward the substrate, matching the flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Class II dihydroorotate oxidase uses flavin-mediated substrate oxidation, fitting the flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed rather than a monooxygenase seed.
Deoxyribonuclease IV is a nuclease hydrolase; nuclease catalysis commonly depends on metal-activated water.
D-amino-acid oxidase uses FAD-mediated amino-acid oxidation, fitting the broad flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Ferredoxin-dependent glutamate synthase includes flavin/iron-sulfur redox transfer, matching the broad flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Bacterial dihydrofolate reductase performs NADPH-dependent hydride transfer without the current flavin, heme, cobalamin, or hydrolase seed chemistry.
Sarcosine oxidase uses covalent or hydride flavin redox chemistry, fitting the flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Trimethylamine dehydrogenase is an FMN and iron-sulfur redox enzyme, matching the broad flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Cytokinin dehydrogenase uses FAD-mediated substrate oxidation, fitting the flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
NAD(P)+ transhydrogenase performs nicotinamide hydride transfer coupled to proton translocation outside the current seed fingerprints.
NADPH-hemoprotein reductase uses FAD/FMN-mediated electron transfer to protein acceptors, matching the flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Urate oxidase is a cofactorless oxidase and does not match the current heme, flavin, cobalamin, or hydrolase seed fingerprints.
Pyruvate:ferredoxin oxidoreductase uses thiamine-diphosphate and iron-sulfur redox chemistry outside the current radical SAM and flavin seed fingerprints.
Ornithine carbamoyltransferase performs carbamoyl transfer chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase uses FAD-linked nicotinamide redox chemistry, matching the flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Sulfite oxidase uses molybdenum-centered sulfite oxidation with heme electron transfer, outside the current heme peroxidase or oxidase seed.
Protein-methionine-S-oxide reductase uses cysteine sulfenic-acid redox chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Adenylyl-sulfate reductase uses FAD and iron-sulfur electron transfer, fitting the broad flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Cytochrome-c oxidase uses heme-copper oxygen reduction chemistry, fitting the heme peroxidase or oxidase seed fingerprint.
Catechol oxidase is a copper-dependent oxidase and does not match the current metal-dependent hydrolase or heme/flavin redox seed fingerprints.
Cytochrome-c3 hydrogenase performs nickel/iron hydrogen redox chemistry outside the current hydrolase, flavin, heme, PLP, and cobalamin seed fingerprints.
Ferredoxin hydrogenase uses iron-sulfur hydrogen redox chemistry rather than the current flavin, heme, cobalamin, PLP, or hydrolase seed mechanisms.
Photinus luciferin monooxygenase performs ATP-dependent luciferyl-adenylate oxygenation outside the current flavin and heme oxygenation seed fingerprints.
Taurine dioxygenase is a non-heme iron and alpha-ketoglutarate dioxygenase, outside the current flavin or heme redox seed fingerprints.
Amine dehydrogenase redox chemistry is not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
Naphthalene 1,2-dioxygenase uses Rieske iron-sulfur and non-heme iron oxygenation chemistry outside the current flavin dehydrogenase seed.
4-hydroxybenzoate 3-monooxygenase is an FAD-dependent aromatic hydroxylase matching the flavin monooxygenase seed fingerprint.
Alkanal monooxygenase is FMN-linked oxygenation chemistry, fitting the broad flavin monooxygenase seed fingerprint.
Camphor 5-monooxygenase is a cytochrome P450 heme oxygenase, fitting the broad heme peroxidase or oxidase seed fingerprint.
Tyrosine 3-monooxygenase uses tetrahydrobiopterin and non-heme iron aromatic hydroxylation outside the current flavin and heme seed fingerprints.
Peptidylglycine monooxygenase is copper-dependent oxygenation chemistry outside the current metal-hydrolase, heme, and flavin seed fingerprints.
Acyl-acyl-carrier-protein desaturase uses diiron oxidative desaturation chemistry outside the current hydrolase and redox seed fingerprints.
Deacetoxycephalosporin-C synthase is a non-heme iron oxidative ring-expansion enzyme outside the current seed fingerprint set.
Superoxide dismutase performs metal-centered radical dismutation rather than metal-activated hydrolysis or the current heme/flavin redox seeds.

Mammalian xanthine dehydrogenase has flavin electron-transfer context, but the M-CSA active site and selected structure are molybdenum-pterin chemistry outside the current local flavin dehydrogenase seed.
Vanadate-dependent chloride peroxidase is peroxidase chemistry but not the heme-dependent peroxidase or oxidase seed fingerprint.

Class II ribonucleoside-triphosphate reductase uses B12-associated radical reduction rather than the current cobalamin radical rearrangement seed, and the selected local active site lacks proximal cobalamin evidence.
4-cresol dehydrogenase contains FAD/heme electron-transfer chemistry and fits the broad flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Ferredoxin-NADP+ reductase is an FAD-dependent hydride/electron-transfer enzyme matching the flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Arsenate reductase uses cysteine-dependent reductive chemistry outside the current metal hydrolase, flavin, heme, PLP, and cobalamin seed fingerprints.
Arsenite oxidase uses molybdenum and iron-sulfur redox chemistry outside the current flavin dehydrogenase and heme seed fingerprints.
Isopenicillin-N synthase is a non-heme iron oxidative ring-forming enzyme outside the current hydrolase and redox seed fingerprints.
Pyrogallol hydroxytransferase uses molybdenum-associated hydroxytransfer chemistry outside the current heme, flavin, hydrolase, PLP, and cobalamin seeds.
Serine hydroxymethyltransferase is a PLP-dependent amino-acid one-carbon transfer enzyme, fitting the PLP-dependent enzyme seed.
Type I transaldolase uses lysine Schiff-base aldol transfer chemistry outside the current PLP, hydrolase, heme, flavin, and cobalamin seeds.
Eukaryotic protein-glutamine gamma-glutamyltransferase uses cysteine acyl-transfer chemistry outside the current Ser-His-Asp/Glu hydrolase seed.
Class B1 beta-lactamase is a metallo-beta-lactamase using metal-activated hydrolysis.
Nucleoside-diphosphate kinase performs nucleotide phosphoryl transfer rather than any current hydrolase, flavin, heme, PLP, or cobalamin seed chemistry.
2-amino-4-hydroxy-6-hydroxymethyldihydropteridine diphosphokinase performs ATP-dependent pyrophosphoryl transfer, not the current metal-hydrolase seed.
Holo-[acyl-carrier-protein] synthase performs phosphopantetheinyl transfer to a serine substrate outside the current hydrolase and redox seed fingerprints.
Thiosulfate sulfurtransferase uses cysteine persulfide sulfur-transfer chemistry outside the current hydrolase, heme, flavin, PLP, and cobalamin seeds.
Estrone sulfotransferase performs PAPS-dependent sulfate transfer rather than the current hydrolase or redox seed mechanisms.
Formyl-CoA transferase uses an aspartyl anhydride acyl-transfer mechanism outside the current metal and Ser-His-Asp/Glu hydrolase seeds.
Coenzyme-B sulfoethylthiotransferase uses nickel-associated radical methyl transfer chemistry outside the current metal-hydrolase and cobalamin seeds.
Hydroxyacylglutathione hydrolase has a binuclear metal center that activates hydroxide for thioester hydrolysis, fitting the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Cerebroside-sulfatase uses metal-assisted sulfate ester hydrolysis with formylglycine chemistry, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Aryldialkylphosphatase uses zinc-activated hydroxide for phosphoester hydrolysis, matching the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Class B1 beta-lactamase is a metallo-beta-lactamase using metal-activated hydrolysis.
Exodeoxyribonuclease III uses magnesium-supported water attack on phosphodiester bonds, fitting the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Crossover junction endodeoxyribonuclease uses metal-stabilized water attack on a phosphate, matching the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Deoxyribonuclease (pyrimidine dimer) uses N-terminal nucleophilic glycosylase/lyase chemistry outside the current hydrolase and redox seed fingerprints.
Ribonuclease HI uses a metal-assisted inline phosphodiester hydrolysis mechanism, fitting the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Ribonuclease 1 uses histidine acid-base transphosphorylation and hydrolysis without the current metal or Ser-His-Asp/Glu hydrolase seed pattern.
Micrococcal nuclease uses calcium-activated water attack on phosphate, matching the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Leukotriene-A4 hydrolase uses zinc-assisted hydrolytic chemistry and peptide hydrolysis, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Bacterial leucyl aminopeptidase uses zinc-activated water to cleave peptide bonds, matching the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Membrane dipeptidase uses metal-assisted water activation for amide hydrolysis, fitting the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Dipeptidyl-peptidase IV uses a Ser-His-Asp catalytic triad and acyl-enzyme intermediate, matching the Ser-His-Asp/Glu hydrolase seed.
Purine nucleoside phosphorylase performs phosphorolysis rather than the current hydrolase seed mechanisms.
Peptidyl-dipeptidase A uses zinc-activated water for peptide-bond hydrolysis, matching the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Carboxypeptidase A uses a zinc/Glu system to activate water for peptide hydrolysis, fitting the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Isoaspartyl dipeptidase uses two zinc ions and activated hydroxide for peptide hydrolysis, matching the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Trypsin uses a Ser-His-Asp catalytic triad and acyl-enzyme hydrolysis mechanism, fitting the Ser-His-Asp/Glu hydrolase seed.
Papain is a cysteine protease with thiolate covalent catalysis, outside the current Ser-His-Asp/Glu and metal-dependent hydrolase seed definitions.
HIV-1 retropepsin is an aspartyl protease using paired Asp acid-base catalysis, outside the current metal and Ser-His-Asp/Glu hydrolase seeds.
Glycine amidinotransferase performs group transfer chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Alpha-mannosidase is a glycosidase but its acid-base glycoside hydrolysis mechanism is not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
Class A beta-lactamase is a serine beta-lactamase but not the Ser-His-Asp/Glu triad hydrolase fingerprint currently defined.
Fumarate reductase has flavin-mediated redox chemistry matching the flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed fingerprint.
Malate dehydrogenase and decarboxylating chemistry is outside the current seed fingerprints.
Aralkylamine N-acetyltransferase performs acetyl transfer chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Glycine N-methyltransferase is a methyltransferase outside the current seed fingerprints.
4-chlorobenzoyl-CoA dehalogenase has covalent dehalogenase chemistry not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
N-carbamoylsarcosine amidase is not confidently mapped to a current seed fingerprint without a more specific amidase fingerprint.
Phosphatidylinositol-specific phospholipase C chemistry is not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
Phospholipase C hydrolyzes phosphodiester bonds and fits a metal-dependent hydrolase seed mechanism in this coarse label set.
Phosphoinositide phospholipase C hydrolyzes a phosphodiester with metal-stabilized water chemistry, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Glutaminase-asparaginase uses N-terminal threonine amidohydrolase chemistry not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
NAD(P)H dehydrogenase quinone chemistry is flavin redox hydride transfer, matching the flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Formate C-acetyltransferase uses glycyl radical transfer chemistry outside the current radical SAM seed fingerprint.
Thymidylate synthase uses folate-dependent methyl transfer and reductive chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Lactoylglutathione lyase uses metal-assisted lyase/isomerization chemistry rather than metal-activated hydrolysis.
Methylmalonyl-CoA epimerase uses metal-associated acid-base epimerization rather than the current hydrolase seed mechanisms.
Catechol 2,3-dioxygenase is a non-heme iron dioxygenase and does not match the current flavin or heme oxygenase seeds.
Phosphorylase kinase performs ATP-dependent phosphoryl transfer outside the current seed fingerprints.
S-2-haloacid dehalogenase uses covalent aspartate dehalogenase chemistry not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
Prostaglandin-endoperoxide synthase has heme-dependent peroxidase and cyclooxygenase redox chemistry, fitting the heme peroxidase or oxidase seed.
GTP cyclohydrolase I uses zinc-assisted water attack in a hydrolytic ring-opening step, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Inosine-uridine nucleoside hydrolase uses divalent-cation-assisted water attack on a glycosidic bond, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Copper nitrite reductase performs metal-centered electron-transfer reduction chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Phosphoglycerate kinase performs magnesium-assisted phosphoryl transfer between ADP and phosphoglycerate rather than hydrolysis or current redox seed chemistry.
Deoxyribonuclease I hydrolyzes phosphodiester bonds with metal-assisted water attack, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Nuclease hydrolyzes phosphodiesters through water attack with metal-associated active-site residues, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Purple acid phosphatase performs metal-assisted phosphate ester hydrolysis, fitting the metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Alkaline phosphatase uses metal-supported phosphate ester hydrolysis and is closest to the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Cyclomaltodextrin glucanotransferase uses glycosyl transfer and glycoside chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Adenine-specific DNA methyltransferase uses SAM-dependent methyl transfer, not the radical SAM or hydrolase seed mechanisms.
Protein-tyrosine phosphatase uses cysteine phosphatase chemistry rather than the current metal-dependent or Ser-His-Asp hydrolase seeds.
Hypoxanthine phosphoribosyltransferase performs phosphoribosyl transfer chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Histidine decarboxylase uses pyruvoyl-dependent decarboxylation chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Carboxypeptidase D uses a Ser-His-Asp catalytic triad for peptide-bond hydrolysis, fitting the Ser-His-Asp/Glu hydrolase seed.
Orotidine-5'-phosphate decarboxylase performs decarboxylation chemistry outside the current hydrolase and redox seed fingerprints.
Phosphoenolpyruvate carboxykinase couples decarboxylation to ATP phosphoryl transfer rather than the current hydrolase or redox seed mechanisms.
Class II fructose-bisphosphate aldolase uses metal-assisted aldol carbon-carbon bond chemistry rather than metal-activated hydrolysis.
Malate synthase combines metal-assisted carbon-carbon bond formation with thioester processing, outside the current coarse hydrolase seed.
Type I 3-dehydroquinate dehydratase uses Schiff-base-assisted dehydration chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Type II 3-dehydroquinate dehydratase performs acid-base dehydration rather than the current heme, hydrolase, or PLP seed mechanisms.
tRNA-pseudouridine synthase performs base rearrangement/isomerization chemistry not represented by the current seed fingerprints.
Nitrile hydratase performs metal-assisted nitrile hydration rather than the ester, amide, or phosphate hydrolysis covered by the current metal hydrolase seed.
Adenylate cyclase performs metal-assisted cyclic phosphate formation from ATP, not metal-activated hydrolysis in the current seed set.
3-dehydroquinate synthase uses NAD-linked redox, elimination, and ring-forming chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Glutathione-disulfide reductase is FAD/NAD(P)H disulfide redox chemistry matching the flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Glucosamine-6-phosphate deaminase performs deamination and tautomerization chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
2-hydroxymuconate tautomerase uses N-terminal proline tautomerase chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Methylmalonyl-CoA mutase uses adenosylcobalamin radical rearrangement chemistry matching the cobalamin radical rearrangement seed.
Methylaspartate mutase uses adenosylcobalamin radical rearrangement chemistry matching the cobalamin radical rearrangement seed.
DNA topoisomerase type III uses DNA strand-cleavage and religation chemistry rather than the current metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Adenylosuccinate synthase performs nucleotide-dependent ligation/addition chemistry outside the current hydrolase and PLP seed fingerprints.
D-alanine transaminase is a pyridoxal-phosphate aminotransferase and fits the current PLP-dependent enzyme seed fingerprint.
Alanine dehydrogenase performs NAD-linked amino acid redox chemistry outside the current PLP and hydrolase seed fingerprints.
Isovaleryl-CoA dehydrogenase is FAD-dependent redox chemistry matching the flavin dehydrogenase or reductase seed.
Acyl-carrier-protein UDP-N-acetylglucosamine O-acyltransferase performs acyl transfer chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Isocitrate dehydrogenase performs NADP-linked oxidation and decarboxylation, not metal-activated hydrolysis in the current seed set.
Methylmalonyl-CoA decarboxylase performs decarboxylation chemistry outside the current hydrolase, PLP, and redox seed fingerprints.
Uridine nucleosidase is a ribosyl hydrolase but lacks the current Ser-His-Asp/Glu hydrolase-triad seed mechanism.
L-fuculose-phosphate aldolase uses aldol carbon-carbon bond chemistry rather than the current metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
4a-hydroxytetrahydrobiopterin dehydratase performs dehydration chemistry outside the current hydrolase and heme seed fingerprints.
Dethiobiotin synthase uses ATP-dependent ring-forming ligase chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Aspartate-ammonia ligase performs ATP-dependent ligation chemistry outside the current hydrolase and PLP seed fingerprints.
NAD+ ADP-ribosyltransferase performs ADP-ribosyl transfer chemistry outside the current hydrolase and redox seed fingerprints.
Acetyl-CoA C-acyltransferase performs carbon-carbon acyl transfer chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Citrate synthase performs CoA thioester condensation chemistry rather than the current hydrolase or redox seed mechanisms.
Nicotinate-nucleotide-dimethylbenzimidazole phosphoribosyltransferase performs phosphoribosyl transfer outside the current seed fingerprints.
Nicotinate-nucleotide diphosphorylase performs phosphoribosyl transfer chemistry outside the current hydrolase and redox seed fingerprints.
Adenylosuccinate lyase performs elimination/lyase chemistry outside the current hydrolase and redox seed fingerprints.
Chorismate mutase performs pericyclic rearrangement chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Glutamine-fructose-6-phosphate transaminase isomerizing performs amidotransfer/isomerization chemistry outside the current PLP seed.
Group IB phospholipase A2 uses calcium-supported phospholipid ester hydrolysis, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
6-pyruvoyltetrahydropterin synthase performs rearrangement/elimination chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Methylglyoxal synthase performs phosphate elimination chemistry outside the current hydrolase and redox seed fingerprints.
Arginine kinase performs ATP-dependent phosphoryl transfer outside the current seed fingerprints.
Urease is a nickel-dependent enzyme catalyzing urea hydrolysis, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
UDP-glucose-hexose-1-phosphate uridylyltransferase performs nucleotide-sugar transfer chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
Pentalenene synthase performs terpene cyclization chemistry outside the current hydrolase and redox seed fingerprints.
Methionine adenosyltransferase performs ATP-dependent adenosyl transfer and triphosphate processing outside the current seed fingerprints.
Adenosylhomocysteinase uses NAD-linked chemistry outside the current flavin, cobalamin, and hydrolase seed fingerprints.
Pyrimidine-nucleoside phosphorylase performs phosphorolysis rather than the current hydrolysis seed mechanisms.
UDP-glucose 6-dehydrogenase performs NAD-dependent oxidation outside the current flavin and cobalamin redox seed fingerprints.
Hydroxymethylglutaryl-CoA reductase performs NADPH-dependent reductive chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
1-alkyl-2-acetylglycerophosphocholine esterase is a serine esterase and fits the Ser-His-Asp/Glu hydrolase seed.
L-fucose isomerase uses metal-associated sugar isomerization rather than metal-activated hydrolysis in the current seed set.
Creatinase hydrolysis is not confidently mapped to the current metal-dependent or Ser-His-Asp/Glu hydrolase seeds from available geometry context.
Cytidine deaminase uses zinc-supported hydrolytic deamination, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Peptide deformylase uses a catalytic metal for deformylation/hydrolysis chemistry, fitting the coarse metal-dependent hydrolase seed.
Methanol dehydrogenase uses PQQ/cytochrome-linked redox chemistry outside the current seed fingerprints.
